questions.clue: Created by Atari engineer Allan Alcorn as a training exercise in 1972, this arcade simulation of table tennis became the first commercially successful video game.

citations.title: Pong

questions.clue: The oldest active venue in Major League Baseball, this Boston stadium opened in 1912 and features a towering left-field wall nicknamed 'the Green Monster.'

citations.title: Fenway Park

questions.clue: In this board game, players fire 'shots' over a dividing mark, trying to guess the positions of their opponent's pieces.

questions.clue: This 1965 Monsanto product spread so widely across professional playing surfaces that its name became the common generic term for all artificial athletic fields.

citations.title: AstroTurf

questions.clue: This NHL franchise played eight seasons in Atlanta before relocating to Canada in 1980 and winning its first and only Stanley Cup title nine years later.
